campaign_name: DS - CWU - Transfer - Reddit Sponsored Post
notes:
Hey Tracy! 

CWU is VERY interested in thorough updates on how we think this campaign is performing. Since we don't have much benchmarking available in the  benchmark dash, could we maybe get some benchmarks from Reddit reps? The client is just looking for a way to understand if it is performing well or not since it's new to them. LMK your thoughts. Thank you!

February Insights:

MoM, conversions decreased 34%. This aligns with a similar trend seen this time last year, suggesting seasonality is a factor, in addition to several campaigns concluding in the prior month.

UG/Transfer PPC: Conversions decreased 16% MoM, driven by a 12% increase in CPCs. The Brand campaign in particular has trended upward in CPA over the past 30 days, so in mid-March the team implemented a Target CPA bid strategy to help guide the platform's bids and improve cost efficiency. Positively, the February CPA of $24 remains well below the agency benchmark of $42.

UG/Transfer Display Retargeting: Performance remained very similar MoM, with a strong CPA that is 56% below the agency benchmark. YoY, performance appears very strong; however, last year at this time there were DSP conversion tracking issues that likely impacted reported performance.

IP Targeting Admits: As expected, CTR declined as the list of admits became more familiar with the ads. The 0.35% CTR is slightly lower than last cycle's 0.40%, but remains above the 0.33% agency benchmark.

Several campaigns resumed or launched in March, and we look forward to monitoring performance as they ramp up.

campaign_name: DS - CWU - UG - IP Targeting - Admits
notes: Refreshed audience to continue targeting most up-to-date list of admitted students.
Switched the campaign's bid strategy and creative optimization from optimizing towards max impressions to max conversions now that it has generated significant conversion data to optimize off of.

January Insights :

Lead Gen campaigns:
- Undergraduate Meta Lead Gen: 15 in-platform leads (flat MoM) at a $178 CPL (+54% MoM, +46% YoY, +242% above the benchmark). This trend in decreasing cost-efficiency is correlated with the additional lead form field (Admit Term) added at the end of October, introducing added friction in the submission process. While this helps improve lead quality, it typically reduces completion rates, which can raise CPL over time. Given that the increase began shortly after this change and has persisted, this is likely a primary factor. We may want to revisit the added form field and evaluate whether the trade-off between lead quality and volume is still meeting goals. We have the option to hardcode the Admit Term in the backend so users don't need to manually select it on the form while still passing that data through. From an optimization standpoint, the Behavioral ad set was delivering a higher CPL compared to the Lookalike ad set, so we've paused Lookalike to prioritize efficiency and help bring overall CPL down.

Conversion Campaigns:
- UG/Transfer PPC: 418 conversions (+20% MoM), following seasonal trends we expect post-holidays, as well as a trend seen this time last year. The $22 CPA is below the agency benchmark of $34.
- Parents Meta: The campaigns concluded, running 8/1/25 - 1/31/26. The campaign was successful, with YoY improvements showing conversions up 45% at a flat CPA. This is largely due to a longer duration and increased budget of the Behavioral campaign, as well as the change from a traffic objective to conversions in the retargeting campaign. If we include last year's Parent Lead Gen campaign in YoY comparisons, the Parent Meta campaigns captured 27% more conversions at a 27% lower CPA.
- Undergraduate Meta Behavioral: The campaign concluded, running 8/1/25 - 1/31/26. The campaign was successful, with YoY improvements showing conversions up 93% at a 55% lower CPA. This is largely due to the switch from a traffic objective to conversions YoY, as well as a decreased cost of impressions, expanding our reach and potential for conversion capture. The video ads were the top conversion drivers, and we recommend including video placements again next cycle.
- UG/Transfer Display Retargeting: Performing very similarly YoY with a nearly flat CPA. However, the CPA is 59% lower than the agency benchmark.

Awareness campaigns:
- IP Targeting Admits: Launched on 1/1 with a .44% CTR, compared to .50% last year, but above the .28% agency benchmark.

campaign_name: DS - CWU - Transfer - Reddit Sponsored Post
notes:
Hey Haley! 

I'm curious if you still think we should launch this as lead gen or if we should do conversions objective based on how other Reddit lead gen campaigns have performed? Thank you!

December Insights Note:

Following up on the note from our last call regarding YoY decreases in PPC performance within Clarity: it is unfortunate to report we are unable to pull alternative data via Slate because the Ping tracking script is not on the PPC landing page.

Given the historical PPC data inaccuracies in the Paid Dashboard and the current tracking gaps impacting Clarity, we will use MoM Paid Dashboard data for PPC performance reporting in the near term, as this should now reflect accurate tracking, comparing performance against agency benchmarks to evaluate success.

Lead Gen campaigns:
- Undergraduate Meta Lead Gen: 15 in-platform leads (-25% MoM). CPL remained relatively flat (+5%). This decrease in leads but relatively similar efficiency is due to a glitch on the Meta platform that caused all Carnegie-managed campaigns to pause from 12/23 - 29. The CPL is 41% above the benchmark, but 17% lower YoY. This campaign isn't currently distinguishable in Clarity for funnel attribution.
- Transfer Meta Lead Gen: Paused at the end of November and will resume in March.

Conversion Campaigns:
- UG/Transfer PPC: 338 conversions (-23% MoM), following seasonal trends we expect with the holidays as well as a trend seen this time last year. The $26 CPA is significantly below the agency benchmark of $74!
- Parents Meta: The behavioral campaign captured conversions at a $27 CPA, above the agency benchmark of $19, but 18% lower YoY. The Retargeting campaign captured sufficient conversion data and switched from a traffic objective to conversions objective on 11/5. With this change, CPA is 76% lower YoY, and is 48% lower than the agency benchmark!
- Undergraduate Meta Behavioral: Conversions declined MoM, following seasonal trends we expect with the holidays as well as a trend seen this time last year. Performance remains efficient with a 53% lower CPA YoY that's also aligned with the agency benchmark.
- UG/Transfer Display Retargeting: Performing very similarly YoY with a nearly flat CPA. However, the CPA is 63% lower than the agency benchmark!
- Transfer Meta Retargeting: Paused at the end of November and will resume in March.

Awareness campaigns:
- YouTube: The campaign ran 8/1-12/31 with the primary goal of serving impressions efficiently. It delivered nearly 3.5x more impressions YoY with only a 16% higher budget. While not primarily focused on conversions, the campaign captured 46, more than 7x last year's total. The View Rate of the 30s ad was 18%, closely aligned with the 20% benchmark.
- IP Targeting: Running 10/1-12/31, this campaign targeted a list of inquiries to encourage applications. CTR remained steady at 0.23% versus 0.24% last year, but application conversions dropped from over 80 last year to 4 this year. We are investigating conversion tracking and will provide an update.
- Spotify: The audio-only campaign ran 8/1-12/31, achieving a 91% completion rate, slightly above last year's 90% but below the 93% agency benchmark. Next cycle, we recommend adding a video ad to expand reach, improve recall, and reduce ad fatigue.
Reviewed search terms and added negative keywords to ensure relevant campaign traffic.

notes:
November Insights Note

Following up on the note from our last call regarding YoY decreases in PPC performance within Clarity, our team identified that the tracking script was not firing on https://www.cwu.edu/page/campaigns/undergrad.php. This campaign page is a standalone file and does not use the university's global template, so it did not automatically inherit the tracking script, which is why it was initially missed. We've updated the GTM rule to include this page. Once your team publishes the updated GTM container, we'll confirm that tracking is fully restored.

Given the historical PPC data inaccuracies in the Paid Dashboard and the current tracking gaps impacting Clarity, we will use monthly Paid Dashboard data for PPC performance reporting in the near term, as this should now reflect accurate tracking. We'll also compare performance against agency benchmarks to evaluate success. In parallel, we'll continue exploring additional ways to assess YoY PPC performance.

Lead Gen campaigns:
- Transfer Meta Lead Gen: 58 in-platform leads (+6% MoM). CPL is 37% below the agency benchmark! The campaign was not live in November last year for YoY comparisons. The campaign paused at the end of November and will resume in March. This campaign isn't currently distinguishable in Clarity for funnel attribution. 
- Undergraduate Meta Lead Gen: 20 in-platform leads (-44% MoM). Admit Term was added as an additional field at the end of October to ensure prospects are placed in the appropriate comm flows after lead capture, and is the cause of the decrease in conversions. If this is a concern, we could hardcode all leads as Fall 2026 so they don't have the additional field. CPL is 69% above the benchmark, but 18% lower YoY. This campaign isn't currently distinguishable in Clarity for funnel attribution.

Conversion Campaigns:
- UG/Transfer PPC: 430 conversions (-4% MoM). The $21 CPA is significantly below the agency benchmark of $60!
- Parents Meta: The behavioral campaign captured conversions at a $32 CPA, right in line with the agency benchmark of $30, but 60% higher YoY. The cost of impressions are up YoY, impacting reach and therefore potential for conversions. The Retargeting campaign captured sufficient conversion data and switched from a traffic objective to conversions objective on 11/5. We don't have sufficient data to form a Parent Retargeting conversion objective agency benchmark, but with this change, CPA is 60% lower YoY!
- Undergraduate Meta Behavioral: Performing well with a 22% lower CPA YoY that's also $6 below agency benchmarks.
- Transfer Meta Retargeting: The CPA is half the agency benchmark! Campaign not live at this time last year for YoY data.
- UG/Transfer Display Retargeting: Performing excellently YoY, with conversions nearly double at half the CPA. A main driver of this success is the campaign had not switched to optimizing toward conversions until early December last year. However, the CPA is also 63% lower than the agency benchmark!

Awareness campaigns:
- YouTube: 3% view rate (716 November true views), below the 6% benchmark. 
- IP Targeting: Launched on 10/1, the CTR decreased 56% MoM, a typical trend with list-based campaigns as early performance captures the most engaged users first, and as frequency increases, click behavior naturally tapers off. 
- Spotify: 92% completion rate in November, just below the 93% benchmark.

Hey Haley!

I see performance dipped in November. Curious if you think it's because we added admit term to the lead form? If the client has concerns, could we offer to hardcode the admit term as Fall 2026? Thank you!

November Insights Note

Following up on the note from our last call regarding YoY decreases in PPC performance within Clarity, our team identified that the tracking script was not firing on https://www.cwu.edu/page/campaigns/undergrad.php. This campaign page is a standalone file and does not use the university's global template, so it did not automatically inherit the tracking script, which is why it was initially missed. We've updated the GTM rule to include this page. Once your team publishes the updated GTM container, we'll confirm that tracking is fully restored.

Given the historical PPC data inaccuracies in the Paid Dashboard and the current tracking gaps impacting Clarity, we will use monthly Paid Dashboard data for PPC performance reporting in the near term, as this should now reflect accurate tracking. We'll also compare performance against agency benchmarks to evaluate success. In parallel, we'll continue exploring additional ways to assess YoY PPC performance.

Lead Gen campaigns:
- Transfer Meta Lead Gen: 58 in-platform leads (+6% MoM). CPL is 37% below the agency benchmark! The campaign was not live in November last year for YoY comparisons. The campaign paused at the end of November and will resume in March. This campaign isn't currently distinguishable in Clarity for funnel attribution. 
- Undergraduate Meta Lead Gen: 20 in-platform leads (-44% MoM). Admit Term was added as an additional field at the end of October to ensure prospects are placed in the appropriate comm flows after lead capture, and is the cause of the decrease in conversions. If this is a concern, we could hardcode all leads as Fall 2026 so they don't have the additional field. CPL is 69% above the benchmark, but 18% lower YoY. This campaign isn't currently distinguishable in Clarity for funnel attribution.

Conversion Campaigns:
- UG/Transfer PPC: 430 conversions (-4% MoM). The $21 CPA is significantly below the agency benchmark of $60!
- Parents Meta: The behavioral campaign captured conversions at a $32 CPA, right in line with the agency benchmark of $30, but 60% higher YoY. The cost of impressions are up YoY, impacting reach and therefore potential for conversions. The Retargeting campaign captured sufficient conversion data and switched from a traffic objective to conversions objective on 11/5. We don't have sufficient data to form a Parent Retargeting conversion objective agency benchmark, but with this change, CPA is 60% lower YoY!
- Undergraduate Meta Behavioral: Performing well with a 22% lower CPA YoY that's also $6 below agency benchmarks.
- Transfer Meta Retargeting: The CPA is half the agency benchmark! Campaign not live at this time last year for YoY data.
- UG/Transfer Display Retargeting: Performing excellently YoY, with conversions nearly double at half the CPA. A main driver of this success is the campaign had not switched to optimizing toward conversions until early December last year. However, the CPA is also 63% lower than the agency benchmark!

campaign_name: Central Washington University - SEO - Copywriting and Optimization
notes:
Organic Traffic Lifetime Performance

Google Search Console (October 1 to December 8):
- From October to early December, our optimized pages generated 123.7k impressions (+13% YoY) and nearly 5k clicks (+99% YoY). 

- For higher-ed institutions, a healthy click-through rate (CTR) is 2.7%, which is our benchmark for overall organic traffic. CWU's CTR is 4.00%, which exceeds our benchmark. Because many of our optimized pages target branded keywords, this boosts your CTR, suggesting strong brand recognition and a sense of trustworthiness.

- The Cost, Aid, and Value page experienced a significant increase in impressions (+6.6% YoY) and organic clicks (+256% YoY).
- Financial Aid and Scholarships also had a drastic improvement in organic visibility and traffic, with impressions growing by +175% and clicks increasing by 391%.

- The Visit page experienced a decrease in visibility (-19%) primarily due to a drop in impressions from "Central Washington University tours" and "CWU campus map." According to the Google Keyword Planner, the search volume for "Central Washington University tours" decreased by 44%. Alternatively, "CWU campus map" gained more searches.

- The Academics page experienced a decline in visibility and traffic, with decreases of 18.4% and 42%, respectively. GKP shows a -50% decrease for "Central Washington University Academics." When users search for "CWU degrees" or "CWU majors," the program finder appears in the top positions, so users still land on the Central Washington website. 
- We recommend updating "H2: Explore Our Academic Departments" to "Explore CWU's Programs and Academic Departments" in an effort to gain a boost in visibility from "CWU program."
- Additionally, there's the option to update the "H2: Discover the Value of a CWU Degree" to "Why Earn Your CWU Degree?" to target more question-based queries, which are increasingly crucial for AI search.

- While there's no historical data to compare, the thought leadership and student stories are also driving organic visibility.
 
Keyword Ranking (October 1 to December 8):

Local Ranking
- Local visibility has grown by nearly 10%.
The following keywords improved in local rankings: 
- "How do I transfer colleges?" and "Steps to transfer colleges?" moved up +99 positions and rank #1.

Branded keywords remained strong and consistently ranked in the first position.

National Ranking
- National visibility increased by 3%. 
-Like local rankings, branded keywords stayed strong and remained in the first ranking position

- Numerous of our optimized pages appear for AI Overviews on both the local level, which helps expand CWU's visibility and SERP real estate.

Google Analytics 4 (October 1 to December 8):
- GA4 shows our content has captured 4.2K total users (+68% YoY), 4k (+72% YoY) engaged sessions, and had an engagement rate of 72%. We typically aim for an engagement rate of 60-70%, so CWU's engagement rate is healthy, indicating that our content is helpful and relevant to users.
- Of the 4.2k users, 1.8k of them were first-visit users, which was a 96% increase from the previous year. They also boasted an 84.7% engagement rate 		
- Additionally, users' engagement is evident in the number of views, which increased by 28% YoY. This indicates that users are exploring the CWU's site further to learn more about program offerings, admissions and aid, thought leadership, and other relevant information. 

- There was also a 23% increase in conversions. While CWU's key events include more generalized actions like "scroll" or "clicks," we saw:
- 90 request information submissions (+62%)
- 31 virtual visit clicks
- 60 account creations

campaign_name: DS - CWU - UG - IP Targeting - Inquiries
notes:
September Insights Note

In September, the paid dashboard shows conversions increased 60% MoM, driven by a 72% lift from PPC. As mentioned last month when PPC conversions were lower than expected, we initiated a tracking investigation. The team identified multiple issues -- the most significant being GTM falling off the Slate instance in August and being added back on 9/4. This explains the lower reported PPC performance in August and the now-elevated MoM growth in September. A follow-up will be shared shortly with more formal language outlining the issues, impact, and required next steps to resolve tracking gaps.

Because of this, we're using Clarity data for YoY reporting. From July 1-September 30, Carnegie (paid media, Clarity, and CollegeXpress) has attributed 225 Fall 2026 application submissions, compared to 196 Fall 2025 at this time last year.

Lead gen campaigns were not impacted by the GTM issue since in-platform tracking remained intact.
- Construction Management Meta: In-platform leads were flat MoM (99). Clarity shows 222 current leads and 2 current pending apps. CPL continues to be highly efficient -- roughly half the non-traditional undergrad benchmark.
- Transfer Meta: 59 in-platform leads (+5% MoM). CPL is 35% below the benchmark and 55% lower YoY. This campaign isn't currently distinguishable in Clarity for funnel attribution.
- Undergraduate Meta: 36 in-platform leads (-18% MoM). The $62 CPL is double the benchmark but remains 80% more efficient YoY. In early October, lower-engaging placements were removed to drive stronger October performance. This campaign isn't currently distinguishable in Clarity for funnel attribution.

Awareness campaigns rely on view and click metrics, which remain valid while conversion tracking is addressed:
- Parents Meta Retargeting: CTR +2% MoM (449 clicks), in line with benchmark.
- Spotify: 90% completion rate (652,271 plays), just below the 93% benchmark. A refresh was launched in early October to re-engage users.
- TikTok: 0.82% CTR, below the 1.02% benchmark. View rate remains strong at 99.3% (75,682 completions).
- YouTube: 4% view rate (22,236 true views), below the 9% benchmark. Additional interest keywords were added in early October to expand reach.

August 2025 Performance Note:

Impressions, clicks, and conversions are all up MoM, largely driven by the launch of several new campaigns on 8/1 (UG TikTok, Parents Meta, UG Meta, Transfer Meta, UG Spotify, UG YouTube). These campaigns are ramping up, giving the platforms and our team valuable data to optimize against.

This cycle is off to a strong start, with conversions up 30% YoY despite a slightly smaller budget. Meta is a major driver of this success, capturing nearly double the conversions at half the CPA compared to last year. Key YoY optimizations include:
- Transfer Meta: Added a lookalike audience based on past lead submitters, expanding reach and efficiency.
- Parents Meta: Shifted to conversion-based optimization instead of in-platform leads, and a shorter lead form (one less field) making it an easier lift for the user to complete.
- UG Meta: Launched with new creative, resulting in nearly double the CTR YoY.

The CMGT & SHM Meta campaigns completed their first full month. The lead gen campaign is performing exceptionally well with a $44 CPL-well below the agency's non-traditional benchmark of $128. Clarity data shows Carnegie campaigns have contributed to 133 current CMGT leads and 1 current pending application.

PPC performance declined MoM, though no major optimizations or competition changes were made to explain the increase in costs. September performance is trending back toward typical levels, and we are continuing to investigate further.

Impressions, clicks, and conversions are all up MoM, largely driven by the launch of several new campaigns on 8/1 (UG TikTok, Parents Meta, UG Meta, Transfer Meta, UG Spotify, UG YouTube). These campaigns are ramping up, giving the platforms and our team valuable data to optimize against.

This cycle is off to a strong start, with conversions up 30% YoY despite a slightly smaller budget. Meta is a major driver of this success, capturing nearly double the conversions at half the CPA compared to last year. Key YoY optimizations include:
- Transfer Meta: Added a lookalike audience based on past lead submitters, expanding reach and efficiency.
- Parents Meta: Shifted to conversion-based optimization instead of in-platform leads, and a shorter lead form (one less field) making it an easier lift for the user to complete.
- UG Meta: Launched with new creative, resulting in nearly double the CTR YoY.

The CMGT & SHM Meta campaigns completed their first full month. The lead gen campaign is performing exceptionally well with a $44 CPL-well below the agency's non-traditional benchmark of $128. Clarity data shows Carnegie campaigns have contributed to 133 current CMGT leads and 1 current pending application.

campaign_name: Central Washington University - UG/Transfer - Display - Retargeting
notes:
June 2025 Reporting Note Part 2:

The remaining campaigns from this cycle all concluded by 6/30. The lifetime notes for each are below/in part 1 of the reporting note.

Transfer TikTok (3/1/25-6/15/25): 
Captured 57 in-platform leads at a $316 CPL. We do not have sufficient data to benchmark against for this audience and time period; however, your platform specialist monitoring the campaign considers this an efficient CPL for a Transfer audience, and an effective way to expand reach beyond Meta and Snapchat (Reddit next cycle) for Transfer lead generation. Next cycle's YoY data will be great for benchmarking!

UG/Transfer Display Retargeting (7/1/24-6/30/25): 
Captured 981 conversions (589 apps, 312 leads, 80 visits) at an average $15 CPA. Looking at the applicable YoY time frame (1/10/2024-4/30/2024 v. 1/10/2025-4/30/2025), conversions are up significantly +114% at a -63% CPA! This year, we added RFI conversion tracking, had more ads in market, in addition to adding Transfer as an audience, which bolstered our reach and engagement. For future flights we recommend continuing with this dual approach, and utilizing more group student imagery.

UG/Transfer PPC (7/1/24-6/30/25): 
As discussed throughout the year, PPC has faced conversion tracking issues. For this reason, Clarity data is a better source of truth. Clarity shows 1,042 users who submitted leads (+34% YoY) and 1,445 users who submitted applications (+726% YoY) can be attributed to the PPC campaign. The significant increase in conversions YoY is attributed to being in market 6 months earlier, an updated landing page, combined in-state and out-of-state geos, and the addition of Transfer keywords. The $65 cost per application is significantly below benchmarks for public institutions of a similar size!

campaign_name: DS - Central Washington University - Transfer - Comp Sci - Mobile Location Targeting
notes:
June 2025 Reporting Note Part 1:

The remaining campaigns from this cycle all concluded by 6/30. The lifetime notes for each are below/in part 2 of the reporting note.

Transfer Comp Sci Meta (3/31/25-6/30/25): 
Captured 98 in-platform leads at a $92 CPL. While we don't have Comp Sci-specific benchmarks, general Transfer campaigns during this period averaged a $67 CPL. It's common for program-specific campaigns to have a higher CPL due to more targeted audiences. To improve performance, we recommend adding vertical videos for Reels/Stories, a placement that's delivered strong lead results with other partners. Additionally, our lookalike ad set generated most of the 98 leads. With this in mind, we'll once again implement lookalike targeting as soon as sufficient lead data is available.

Transfer Comp Sci Mobile Location Targeting (3/28/25-6/30/25): 
Served 613K impressions and captured a .04% CTR. We do not have sufficient data to benchmark for this program, but the average CTR for general Transfer during this time was .07%. Lower CTRs are typical for program-specific Transfer campaigns, given their narrower targeting. Due to the 24hr attribution window of this tactic, conversion visibility is limited as most display ads produce view-through conversions that happen after that window; however, 6 application submissions were captured!

Columbia Basin College was served the highest volume of impressions, trickling down to the highest volume of clicks and conversions. Of the campuses served more than 1K impressions, Highline College captured the highest CTR at .17%. Several campuses were not engaged, with 0 clicks. You can review the complete geo performance breakdown here: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1PlTF-GizzkEv9ZLskRM8b5VbBvZYLlGIR2gacTFsT0Y/edit?usp=sharing

campaign_name: DS - Central Washington University - Transfer - Comp Sci - Mobile Location Targeting
notes:
May 2025 Performance:

In May, the general Transfer Meta, Snapchat, and Mobile Location Targeting campaigns concluded. The lifetime performance of each can be found in a separate note.

Conversion volume increased significantly in May due to several optimizations and updates:
- PPC: The DSP-related conversion tracking issue was resolved in late April, resulting in a 7X increase in PPC conversions MoM. This spike reflects the restoration of tracking rather than a sudden surge in performance. Additionally, the RFI form was updated by CWU to prevent double-counting. However, we suspect inflation may still remain. While our team explores tracking solutions, we recommend using Clarity data as the source of truth. According to Clarity, PPC attributed 35 RFIs (-3 MoM), 13 application submissions (flat MoM), and 7 visit registrations (-5 MoM).
- Transfer Meta Lead Gen: Early May, low-performing ad sets were paused, and budget was shifted to the top-performing lookalike audience ad. This adjustment significantly boosted in-platform lead volume 4X MoM.
- Transfer Comp Sci Meta Lead Gen: A new lookalike audience based on past lead submitters launched in early May and quickly became the top-performing audience, helping boost in-platform leads +17% MoM. As a result, the behavioral audience was paused at the end of the month to prioritize budget where performance is strongest.

The Snapchat Transfer In-Platform Lead Generation campaign ran 3/1/25 - 5/31/25, capturing 23 in-platform leads at a $522 Cost Per In-Platform Lead, nearly double agency benchmarks despite high-quality creative and consistent refreshes to keep users engaged. Next cycle, we are prioritizing Transfer budget in higher performing tactics like Meta Lead Gen, as well as testing Reddit Lead Gen as its niche communities and interest-based targeting offer strong potential to reach engaged, college-searching transfer students efficiently.

In May, the general Transfer Meta, Snapchat, and Mobile Location Targeting campaigns concluded. The lifetime performance of each can be found in a separate note.

Conversion volume increased significantly in May due to several optimizations and updates:
- PPC: The DSP-related conversion tracking issue was resolved in late April, resulting in a 7X increase in PPC conversions MoM. This spike reflects the restoration of tracking rather than a sudden surge in performance. Additionally, the RFI form was updated by CWU to prevent double-counting. However, we suspect inflation may still remain. While our team explores tracking solutions, we recommend using Clarity data as the source of truth. According to Clarity, PPC attributed 35 RFIs (-3 MoM), 13 application submissions (flat MoM), and 7 visit registrations (-5 MoM).
- Transfer Meta Lead Gen: Early May, low-performing ad sets were paused, and budget was shifted to the top-performing lookalike audience ad. This adjustment significantly boosted in-platform lead volume 4X MoM.
- Transfer Comp Sci Meta Lead Gen: A new lookalike audience based on past lead submitters launched in early May and quickly became the top-performing audience, helping boost in-platform leads +17% MoM. As a result, the behavioral audience was paused at the end of the month to prioritize budget where performance is strongest.

campaign_name: Central Washington University - Transfer - Mobile Location Targeting
notes:
Transfer Mobile Location Targeting Lifetime Performance:

The Transfer Mobile Location Targeting campaign ran from 3/1/25-5/31/25, using precise geo-targeting to serve ads directly to prospects at top feeder community college campuses. The campaign drove strong awareness, delivering 1.8M impressions with a 0.07% CTR, in line with agency benchmarks. Despite a short 24-hour attribution window, the campaign still captured 39 application submissions and 5 visit registrations, demonstrating its effectiveness in reaching high-intent audiences. 

Of all the zip codes directly associated with the schools that we're targeting, Yakima Valley College captured the largest volume of clicks (880) at a CTR above the average at .08%. Wenatchee Valley College contributed the highest volume of conversions at 9, despite only capturing 16 clicks.

Meta Transfer Lifetime Performance:

The Transfer Meta campaign ran in 2 flights, 7/31/24 - 10/25/24 and 3/3/25 - 5/31/25, and contributed 72% of conversions from the transfer-specific campaigns this fiscal year through May. The tactic included two campaigns, an in-platform lead generation campaign and a retargeting campaign to promote applications. 

The lead gen campaign successfully captured 213 in-platform leads at an efficient average $56 Cost Per In-Platform Lead (CPL), well below agency benchmarks for both flights! Vertical video placements (Stories/Reels) and lookalike audiences were the top lead-drivers and should be continued next cycle.

The retargeting campaign focused on driving past site visitors and/or lead submitters from the Meta Lead Gen campaign back to the site with an Apply Now CTA, garnering over 3K clicks at an average .43% Click Through Rate (CTR)--about 14% lower than agency benchmarks. The retargeting campaign captured 42 applications, 33 leads, and 11 visit registrations. The ads featuring messaging around guaranteed admission were top conversion-driving, while the messaging featuring the 40% of Wildcats are transfer students stat had slightly stronger CTRs.

notes: Refreshed the admits list to continue marketing to the most up-to-date admits promoting deposits and enrollment actions.

In March, the general UG and Transfer campaigns drove increased impressions, clicks, and conversions, fueled by the launch of three new Transfer tactics (Mobile Location Targeting, TikTok, and Snapchat) and the return of two Transfer Meta campaigns (Lead Gen and Retargeting). While still ramping up, collecting user data and receiving optimizations, they are off to a strong start aligning closely with or surpassing our internal benchmarks. 

As discussed last month, PPC conversion tracking issues persist, limiting visibility in the Paid Dashboard. However, Clarity shows that 1,546 new users from PPC campaigns in March completed 51 inquiries, 24 visit registrations, and 18 application submissions. Since Google Ads isn't capturing these conversions, the platform's ability to optimize based on conversion data is impacted. That said, conversion tracking for Display Retargeting was fixed mid-March, contributing to a +73% MoM surge in conversions!

The IP Targeting campaign continues to support yield efforts, delivering 114K+ impressions in March. As expected, CTR has leveled off as users become familiar with the ads. That said, the CTR is still outperforming our internal benchmark!

campaign_name: Central Washington University - UG/Transfer - Display - Retargeting
notes:
In February, the conversion performance from the UG/Transfer PPC and Display Retargeting campaigns dropped significantly MoM. While we expected a bit a of a slow down in performance due to seasonality, this launched an investigation into whether or not there is a conversion tracking concern. Our team discovered the website's Content Security Policy (CSP), a security feature that controls which resources (like scripts and styles) can be loaded on a webpage, is blocking necessary conversion tracking URLs from loading, preventing many conversions from the PPC and Display Retargeting campaigns from being captured. 

The CWU team received context from Carnegie about the situation, so they may take action to resolve the issue in their backend.

campaign_name: Central Washington University - UG/Transfer - Display - Retargeting
notes:
In February, the UG and Transfer campaigns captured -91% conversions MoM, largely due to seasonal shifts in audience intent, the conclusion of the Meta Parents campaign mid-month (lifetime insights recorded separately), and the ending of several tactics in January. Many high school seniors have already submitted their applications, while underclassmen are only beginning their search. Similarly, Transfer prospects typically see heightened engagement in January, influenced by New Year momentum, before returning to typical levels in February.

With the audiences' shift in interest and intent, lower search volume drove up PPC costs, with CPCs increasing +65% MoM, limiting clicks and contributing to the average -93% decline in PPC conversions MoM. Our team continues to refine keyword strategies to help navigate these seasonal fluctuations and is conducting a PPC conversion tracking audit to ensure the downturn isn't linked to a tracking matter.

The IP Targeting campaign proceeds to encourage admits to accept their admission, garnering over 165k impressions in February. Despite the awareness-focused nature of this tactic and limited 24-hour attribution window (conversions are only counted if they happen within 24 hours of viewing the ad), the campaign captured 6 visits and 4 leads in February, indicating strong intent from admits.

We look forward to March performance, including the Transfer Snapchat, TikTok, and Mobile Location Targeting campaigns that launched 3/1!

campaign_name: Central Washington University - UG - IP Targeting - Yield
notes:
In February, the UG and Transfer campaigns captured -91% conversions MoM, largely due to seasonal shifts in audience intent, the conclusion of the Meta Parents campaign mid-month (lifetime insights recorded separately), and the ending of several tactics in January. Many high school seniors have already submitted their applications, while underclassmen are only beginning their search. Similarly, Transfer prospects typically see heightened engagement in January, influenced by New Year momentum, before returning to typical levels in February.

With the audiences' shift in interest and intent, lower search volume drove up PPC costs, with CPCs increasing +65% MoM, limiting clicks and contributing to the average -93% decline in PPC conversions MoM. Our team continues to refine keyword strategies to help navigate these seasonal fluctuations and is conducting a PPC conversion tracking audit to ensure the downturn isn't linked to a tracking matter.

The IP Targeting campaign proceeds to encourage admits to accept their admission, garnering over 165k impressions in February. Despite the awareness-focused nature of this tactic and limited 24-hour attribution window (conversions are only counted if they happen within 24 hours of viewing the ad), the campaign captured 6 visits and 4 leads in February, indicating strong intent from admits.

We look forward to March performance, including the Transfer Snapchat, TikTok, and Mobile Location Targeting campaigns that launched 3/1!

Meta Parents Campaigns - Lifetime Performance:

The Meta Parents Behavioral Conversions campaigns successfully captured 306 conversions at a 4.67% conversion rate, significantly higher than our internal benchmark, indicating strong audience targeting, messaging and creative compatibility.

The Meta Parents Retargeting Traffic campaign generated 610k impressions with a .45% CTR, slightly below our internal benchmark of .56%.

The top performing image for both the conversions and traffic campaigns featured parents with a student at a school computer. To build on this, we recommend adding 1-2 new creatives featuring parents in the next campaign. This will allow us to A/B test messaging while also ensuring that users don't feel like they're seeing the same upper-funnel ads after already submitting an RFI form.

campaign_name: Central Washington University - UG - Snapchat - Lead Gen
notes:
For next cycle Media Planning. Snapchat UG underperformed. Here are insights to decide whether this should run next cycle:

Here are the factors that contributed to this campaign's low performance: 
- Audience Size - Due to the geo targeting, our behavioral ad set's audience size was only 19-21k users. For reference, our best practice is typically around 1M users--Snapchat campaigns thrive when their audiences have enough volume! To help with our audience size, reach, and engagement, we implemented a Lookalike ad set. However, the Lookalike ad set had a similarly small audience size (again, due to the geos): 81-84k users. 
--- Recommendation: If we run this campaign in the future, I strongly recommend targeting all of Washington, Oregon, and Idaho to provide our audience the volume it needs.
- Lead Form Fields - We added the "Admit Term" form field on 10/4, which resulted in a 15.64% decrease in clicks and 100% decrease in leads period over period. This indicates that the majority of prospective students may feel like it's a big commitment to select a term, even though the client is just trying to gauge interest/availability. 
--- Recommendation: If we run this campaign again, I recommend keeping our lead form fields to a minimum--first name, last name, and email--to help boost lead volume. 
- We unfortunately can't pull performance data by county or zip code in Snapchat. However, I can see that the Portland metro area performed best in terms of click volume and CPC efficiency.
- Due to limited audience targeting options, we have super limited audience/demographic insights available too. The top lifestyle category that users in this audience fell into is the "Gamers" category based on click volume, but I wouldn't recommend targeting only the "Gamers" lifestyle next cycle since it would shrink our audience below best practice and risk excluding users who are interested in CWU.
- This cycle's creative was great and I don't think we need to be too worried about it playing a role in the low lead volume. The top-performing video by lead volume was Video A, which included quick, back-to-back clips of students holding "#FutureWildcat" signs. This creative generated all of this campaign's leads. 
--- Recommendation: The client has quite a few strong vertical videos on their organic Instagram page. These videos would work well as ad creative in future Snapchat campaigns:
Future Wildcat Video https://www.instagram.com/reel/C3JZk3tvJLF/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link&igsh=MzRlODBiNWFlZA==
Campus Sunset https://www.instagram.com/reel/DBCICAqNfn8/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link&igsh=MzRlODBiNWFlZA==
Wildcat Pride https://www.instagram.com/reel/C3tay2xsB1G/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link&igsh=MzRlODBiNWFlZA==

In January, the UG and Transfer campaigns garnered improvements in conversions, increasing by 23% MoM. This rebound came after a slow month in December due to seasonal fluctuations. Notably, there were increases in RFI completions (+28%) and visit registrations, which surged by 220%. However, application submissions declined by 6% compared to the previous month, likely due to the start of the Winter term on 1/07.

The Meta UG and Parents campaigns generated significant improvements in conversion volume (+89%) MoM, acquiring 121 total conversions. 
- With a 60% increase in budget MoM, the UG campaigns obtained a noteworthy increase in performance, gathering 35 conversions (an increase of 23 from the month prior) at a reduced CPA (-45%). After our team paused the Behavioral Feed ad set in the Lead Gen campaign on 1/02 to allow more budget for the higher-performing Lookalike and Stories ad sets, conversions more than doubled in these placements MoM. 
- Likewise, conversions for the Parents campaign soared by 65% MoM, securing 86 conversions at a lowered CPA of $35 (-40%). Our team's optimization to remove low-performing placements in the Retargeting campaign on 1/02 also contributed to this success, resulting in an additional 15 conversions MoM while significantly reducing the CPA by 72%.

The Display Outreach campaign to Spanish Speakers concluded at the end of January. The campaign, which was live from 8/01/24 to 1/31/25, drove over 18.5M impressions and secured 52 total conversions. While the 0.09% CTR performed below the Undergraduate CTR benchmark of 0.53%, this is likely due to targeting such a niche audience.
Recommendation: To help improve engagement in future campaigns, we recommend layering in an additional creative ad set. Additionally, we suggest A/B testing one creative element (either the copy, imagery, or CTA) to find what best resonates with prospective students. 

As a reminder, the Parents Meta campaign will conclude in mid-February. We look forward to covering its lifetime performance in the following note.
